skill.name: video-extend
skill.description: Extend an existing video clip with RunComfy by routing a source video URL and continuation prompt to Google Veo 3-1 extend-video endpoints through the runcomfy CLI.
skill.summary: Continue Veo video clips through RunComfy with guided endpoint selection and prompting patterns.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no prompt injection attempt, malware behavior, or hidden exfiltration in SKILL.md. Most Ruby backtick and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives from Markdown and prose. The confirmed risks are legitimate but material: the skill invokes the runcomfy CLI, sends video URLs and prompts to RunComfy, and documents local API token storage.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash(runcomfy *) and provides runcomfy run examples. This is expected for the skill, but it executes an external CLI and should remain constrained to runcomfy subcommands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and invoke examples directly show runcomfy CLI execution. The command scope is narrow, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Submission of User Media
medium_findings.description: The skill sends a source video URL and prompt to RunComfy model endpoints. This is core functionality, but users should understand that media references and prompts leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented command takes video_url and prompt fields and the security section describes POSTing to the RunComfy Model API. This confirms intentional third-party network use.

medium_findings.title: Local Token File and Generated Output Writes
medium_findings.description: The documentation states that runcomfy login writes an API token to a hidden config path and that generated clips are downloaded to an output directory. This is legitimate CLI behavior, but it touches sensitive local files and large generated media files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and output directory behavior are explicitly documented. The file mode and size cap reduce risk, but filesystem access is still confirmed.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Prose
low_findings.description: Ruby backtick execution alerts are Markdown inline code and fenced shell examples, not Ruby execution. Weak cryptography alerts appear to come from product wording and exit-code text; no cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the referenced lines shows Markdown formatting, example commands, and descriptive text. I found no Ruby code or weak cryptographic API usage in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Documented Hardcoded URLs Are Expected
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to RunComfy documentation, model catalog pages, and example user-hosted video locations. No unrelated endpoint or covert destination was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation and catalog links consistent with the stated RunComfy purpose. The example video URL is a placeholder, not an exfiltration endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Pattern for runcomfy CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses Bash(runcomfy *) and runcomfy run commands. Keep the tool allowlist narrow and do not broaden it to arbitrary shell commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell boundary is explicit in the skill metadata and examples. The restricted command prefix limits blast radius but still requires user trust in the CLI.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network API Pattern for Media Generation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill routes source video URLs and prompts to RunComfy model APIs and downloads generated clips. This is intended behavior but should be disclosed to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly says the CLI POSTs to the RunComfy Model API and lists the allowed outbound endpoints. No hidden network path was found.

content.value_statement: Short generated clips often need a clean continuation without rebuilding the whole scene. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to extend Veo clips through RunComfy with focused prompts and endpoint choices.
actual_capabilities: Selects the standard or fast Veo 3-1 extend-video endpoint based on quality and cost needs.
Builds runcomfy CLI commands using a source video URL and continuation prompt.
Guides prompts that describe only the next action after the source clip.
Supports chained extensions by feeding each generated clip into the next request.
Documents expected exit codes and troubleshooting references for RunComfy CLI failures.
Explains privacy boundaries for API tokens, outbound endpoints, and generated file downloads.
limitations: Requires a working runcomfy CLI installation and a valid RunComfy account or token.
Requires an existing video URL; it does not create the first clip from scratch.
Long chains can accumulate identity drift and may need a new image-to-video anchor.
Generated video quality, duration, cost, and latency depend on RunComfy and Veo service behavior.

use_cases.title: Extend a Final Hero Clip
use_cases.description: Continue a polished Veo clip with matching motion, lighting, and subject identity for a longer final asset.

use_cases.title: Draft Multi-Shot Story Beats
use_cases.description: Use fast extensions to test several continuation beats before spending more on a final render.

use_cases.title: Prototype Narrative Chains
use_cases.description: Build a longer sequence by extending one short source clip step by step with focused action prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Extend One Clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple continuation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend this video using the standard Veo 3-1 endpoint. Use this video URL: [video URL]. Continue with: [one clear action beat].

prompt_templates.title: Use Fast Draft Mode
prompt_templates.scenario: Cost-sensitive iteration before a final render.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fast extend-video endpoint for a draft. Source video: [video URL]. The next beat is: [motion, subject action, camera behavior].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Chained Sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: Several continuations from one source clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan three short extend-video calls from this clip: [video URL]. Keep each call to one beat and explain which output becomes the next input.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Final Continuation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt refinement for production output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a final RunComfy extend command for this source: [video URL]. Preserve identity and lighting, anchor the camera, and avoid adding unrelated background motion.

output_examples.input: Extend a source clip where a character looks at an object on a table.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects the standard extend endpoint and writes a continuation prompt where the camera stays steady while the character reaches for the object.

output_examples.input: Create a low-cost draft for the next story beat from an existing Veo clip.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses the fast endpoint, keeps the prompt to one action beat, and directs the output to a local folder.

output_examples.input: Turn one short clip into a longer sequence with multiple extensions.
output_examples.output: The assistant breaks the sequence into separate beats and explains how each generated clip becomes the next source video.

best_practices: Keep each extension prompt focused on one main action beat.
Describe camera behavior clearly so the continuation does not drift.
Use fast mode for drafts and standard mode for final delivery.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to create a first clip without a source video URL.
Do not combine many scene changes into one extension prompt.
Do not provide private or unapproved video URLs to a third-party generation service.

faq.question: Does this skill generate video from text alone?
faq.answer: No. It extends an existing video clip. Use a text-to-video or image-to-video workflow for the first clip.

faq.question: Which RunComfy endpoint does it use?
faq.answer: It uses Google Veo 3-1 extend-video or the fast extend-video variant through the runcomfy CLI.

faq.question: Can it make very long videos?
faq.answer: It can chain extensions, but quality and identity can drift after several generations.

faq.question: Does it upload my video file?
faq.answer: The command sends a video URL and prompt to RunComfy. Use only media you are allowed to process there.

faq.question: Does it require credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. The runcomfy CLI requires login or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: Is the fast endpoint always best?
faq.answer: No. Fast mode is useful for drafts. Standard mode is better for final-quality continuations.
